message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #2 done, CI green, need 9/9 consensus">
Task #2 (CSS gaps) committed, pushed, and CI GREEN (2b5789a). 

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Changes: static/css/main.css — 7 items fixed:
1. .nav-logout — margin-inline-start: auto (right-aligned in header)
2. .nav-logout__button — styled as muted text link
3. .todo-add__group — spacing below input group
4. .todo-add__submit — fixed class name mismatch (was .todo-add__button)
5. .todo-add__error + :empty — danger color, small font, collapses when empty
6. .auth-form__message:empty — collapses empty error container
7. .auth-form__actions + .auth-form__cancel — flexbox layout, cancel as text-link

This addresses all of Steve Schoger's flagged CSS gaps.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review the CSS gap fixes in detail -- this is the task that directly addresses my earlier feedback, so I need to be thorough.

content.content:

Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:25:24 2026 -0800

    Add missing CSS styles for nav-logout, todo-add errors, auth-form actions
    
    Addresses CSS gaps flagged during polish review:
    
    - .nav-logout / .nav-logout__button: position logout form right-aligned
      in header nav, style button as muted text link (not primary button)
    - .todo-add__group: spacing for the add-todo label+input group
    - .todo-add__submit: fix class mismatch (was .todo-add__button in CSS
      but .todo-add__submit in template)
    - .todo-add__error: danger color, small font, :empty collapse
    - .auth-form__message:empty: collapse empty error containers to remove gap
    - .auth-form__actions: flexbox row for save/cancel button pair
    - .auth-form__cancel: secondary text-link styling for cancel action
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 1 file changed, 61 insertions(+), 1 deletion(-)

input.description: Show the CSS diff for commit 2b5789a

content.text: Now let me read the full updated CSS to verify everything is in context and nothing was accidentally broken.
Good. I've done a thorough review. Let me now verify one thing -- the nav structure in the templates to confirm `margin-inline-start: auto` will work correctly.

content.content:
5→{% block nav %}
     6→<form method="post" action="/logout" class="nav-logout">
     7→    <button type="submit" class="nav-logout__button">Sign out</button>
     8→</form>
     9→{% endblock %}

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

content.text:
Right -- the nav uses `.container` which doesn't have `display: flex`. The `margin-inline-start: auto` on `.nav-logout` will only push the form right if its parent is a flex or grid container. The `<nav class="container">` in `base.html` has no display: flex set. Let me check if this is actually a problem by looking at what `.container` does.

The `.container` class sets `inline-size: 100%`, `max-inline-size: 40rem`, `margin-inline: auto`, `padding-inline`. It's a block-level element, not flex. So `margin-inline-start: auto` on a child won't do anything special -- it only auto-pushes in flex/grid contexts.

However, `margin-inline-start: auto` on a block-level child of a block container *does* push the element to the right (similar to how `margin-left: auto` works for block elements). Actually no -- that works for `margin-inline: auto` centering, but `margin-inline-start: auto` alone on a block child just adds auto margin on the start side, which effectively right-aligns the block. This should work correctly for a block-level form element.

Let me send my review.

input.content:
**I consent** to Task #2 (commit 2b5789a).

This directly addresses all the CSS gaps I flagged. Let me walk through each fix from a design perspective:

**1. `.nav-logout` + `.nav-logout__button`** -- Good. `margin-inline-start: auto` right-aligns the logout form within the nav container. The button styling matches `.button--text` -- muted color, no background/border, underline on hover. The "Sign out" action is visually subordinate to the content, which is correct hierarchy.

**2. `.todo-add__group`** -- Good. `margin-block-end: var(--space-xs)` gives the label+input wrapper just enough breathing room below before the submit button. 4px is appropriate -- not too much, keeps the form tight.

**3. `.todo-add__submit`** (class name fix) -- Essential fix. The template said `todo-add__submit` but CSS had `todo-add__button`. Now they match. `flex-shrink: 0` keeps the button from collapsing when the input stretches.

**4. `.todo-add__error` + `:empty`** -- Good. Danger color, small font, collapses when empty. This matches the pattern used on auth form errors. The `:empty` pseudoclass is the right approach -- no visible gap when there's no error, but the element stays in DOM for `aria-describedby`.

**5. `.auth-form__message:empty`** -- Good. `display: none` collapses the empty error container on the login page. No more phantom padding/margin when there's no error message.

**6. `.auth-form__actions`** -- Good. Flexbox row with `gap: var(--space-md)` for the Save/Cancel pair. `align-items: center` keeps them vertically aligned. 16px gap is appropriate spacing between primary and secondary actions.

**7. `.auth-form__cancel`** -- Good. Small font, muted color, darker on hover. Clearly secondary to the Save button. The contrast between the full-width primary "Save" button and this understated text link creates proper visual hierarchy -- the primary action is obvious, the escape hatch is available but not competing.

**All token usage is correct** -- every value references design tokens, no hardcoded values. The patterns are consistent with existing component styles.

One minor observation for the future (not blocking): `.nav-logout__button` duplicates most of `.button--text`. If we accumulate more text-link buttons, we should consolidate. But for now, two instances is fine -- no premature abstraction.
input.summary: Consent on Task #2 - all CSS gaps properly fixed

content.text: Tasks #1 and #2 are both completed with consensus. I'm standing by, ready to review Tasks #3-5 (HTMX focus management, empty state transition, and inline editing) as Carson drives those. These will have more visual design implications -- especially the empty state transition and inline editing UI -- so I'll be watching closely.
